system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
    div(phi,rho)    Gauss linear;
    div(phi,p)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) minmod;
    reconstruct(U)  minmod;
    reconstruct(T)  minmod;
    reconstruct(p)  minmod;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is straightforward:

```
Unknown discretisation scheme minmod
```

The `fvSchemes` file references `minmod` as the limiter for reconstruction schemes, but `minmod` is not in the list of valid schemes shown in the error output. The valid schemes include `Minmod` (capital M), not `minmod` (lowercase).

OpenFOAM 10 is case-sensitive. The reconstruction schemes in `interpolationSchemes` specify `minmod` (all lowercase), but the actual valid scheme name is `Minmod` (capitalized first letter).

## Resolution

**In `system/fvSchemes`**, within the `interpolationSchemes` sub-dictionary, change:

```
reconstruct(rho) minmod;
reconstruct(U)  minmod;
reconstruct(T)  minmod;
reconstruct(p)  minmod;
```

to:

```
reconstruct(rho) Minmod;
reconstruct(U)  Minmod;
reconstruct(T)  Minmod;
reconstruct(p)  Minmod;
```

Similarly, if `reconstruct(U)` uses `minmodV` (as seen in some tutorials), that should also be capitalized as `MinmodV`.

The same applies to any other reconstruction entries — the scheme name must exactly match what is registered in the OpenFOAM scheme registry, which uses `Minmod` (not `minmod`) and `MinmodV` (not `minmodV`).

No other files need modification. This is purely a spelling/case issue in the `fvSchemes` file.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 1.1m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/fvSchemes', changes='Change reconstruct(rho) minmod to reconstruct(rho) Minmod; Change reconstruct(U) minmod to reconstruct(U) Minmod; Change reconstruct(T) minmod to reconstruct(T) Minmod; Change reconstruct(p) minmod to reconstruct(p) Minmod')]
